## Cycle 292 ‚Äî kids build (WIDE orchestration, 3 explored) ‚Äî 284-kids-thunder-drum

**When**: 2026-06-03 ~06:15 UTC (every-2h autonomous cycle).

**Git sync**: origin/main had been **force-updated** again (local main diverged 50/50 on fetch; shallow clone ‚Üí `--ff-only` aborts). Reset local `main --hard origin/main` ‚Üí HEAD `4e13376` (cycle 291, `283-piano-isosurface`). Worked directly on main. `node_modules` ABSENT ‚Üí `npm ci` (exit 0) before the authoritative builds. Highest committed folder = **283** ‚Üí pre-allocated **284/285/286** for the three builders (no collision).

**Love signal** (`/api/dream/votes`, 32 loved): for a kids cycle the relevant pull is the **embodied / sensorimotor + non-standard-instrument** cluster ‚Äî `83-kids-tilt-rain`‚ù§Ô∏è, `140-kids-string-bridge`‚ù§Ô∏è, `133-kids-ripple-pond`‚ù§Ô∏è, `105-pluck-field`‚ù§Ô∏è (its KS/physical-pluck timbre), `166-kids-lantern`‚ù§Ô∏è. The winner (strike a real drum *skin* and feel its physics) sits in this "the instrument is a physical thing you act on, not a button" vein, and is the deepest answer yet to the standing JURY "audit the SOUND" mandate.

**Research-first dive** (mandate; RESEARCH ¬ß292, 2026-06-03 ‚Äî web tools healthy): **real-time physical-modeling / modal synthesis**. Anchor: *nlm: Real-Time Non-linear Modal Synthesis in Max* (**arXiv 2603.10240, March 2026**) ‚Äî playable real-time modal synthesis of strings, **membranes, and plates**, with non-linear (tension/amplitude-dependent) behavior. Foundational cross-refs: circular-membrane **Bessel modes** (Rayleigh, *Theory of Sound*, 1877), tympani **tension-nonlinearity pitch-glide** (Morrison & Rossing), **stick-slip friction** excitation (In√°cio/Antunes/Wright 2008), mass-spring/Verlet **soft-body** (Provot 1995 / M√ºller PBD). Surprise/hook: the lab has 130+ kids instruments but their voice is almost always a **Karplus pluck / triangle bell / additive sine in C-major pentatonic** ‚Äî and modal synthesis attacks that at the root: you choose a *physical object*, and its eigenmodes are inharmonic/**non-pentatonic by physics**, with a body-level "hit harder ‚Üí it bends" intuition a 4-yo already has. Grep confirmed the gap: cymatics (`19`,`165`) only *draw* Chladni/Bessel (visualizers, not playable); Karplus is waveguide *strings*; **zero playable membrane/plate modal synths, zero friction/stick-slip, zero mass-spring‚Üíaudio** in the lab. Chain dive ‚Üí build is direct: ¬ß292 ‚Üí three WIDE briefs (membrane / bowl / jelly) ‚Üí shipped `284-kids-thunder-drum` (research-first rule path (a)).

**Decided**: Kids cycle (292 % 2 = 0). No blocker, nothing in-progress. The standing JURY (2026-06-02) demand is *"audit the SOUND ‚Äî the most monotonous axis; force a non-pentatonic mode; ban the glow/pentatonic."* Recent kids fires broadened the *tuning* (slendro `268`, JI-beating `272`, Bohlen‚ÄìPierce `276`, Lydian `280`) ‚Äî this cycle goes one level deeper and broadens the **synthesis method itself** (physical modeling), where the non-pentatonic pitches fall out of the physics rather than a chosen scale. The live **diversity audit BANS canvas2d-output (5√ó)**, so all three briefs use non-canvas2d output.

**Mode**: **WIDE** (research anchor opened three orthogonal physical objects; JURY "audit/diversify the sound" ‚Üí go WIDE with fresh tags; alternation: 288 WIDE ‚Üí 290 WIDE ‚Üí 291 WIDE ‚Üí keep diversifying since the jury is still flagging convergence, not asking to DEEPEN one piece). Three briefs = three physical objects √ó three never-used techniques √ó three non-canvas2d outputs, all non-pentatonic:
- `284-kids-thunder-drum` ‚Äî **touch strike** ¬∑ **three.js** Bessel-mode membrane mesh ¬∑ **non-linear modal membrane synthesis** (tension pitch-glide). **‚Üê WINNER.**
- `285-kids-singing-bowl` ‚Äî **touch circular-rub** ¬∑ **three.js** metallic bowl + cymatic water ¬∑ **friction/stick-slip modal excitation** (sustain-by-gesture).
- `286-kids-jelly-choir` ‚Äî **touch poke/squish** ¬∑ **inline SVG** wobble blobs ¬∑ **mass-spring/Verlet soft-body ‚Üí audio** (JI harmony).

**Ambition floor** ‚Äî winner cleared **4 of 5** (need 2): `ambition: novel-technique + named-reference + ‚â•3-subsystems + recent-research(¬ß292)`
- **(1) Novel technique** ‚Äî grepped READMEs+INDEX: cymatics only *draw* membrane modes; Karplus is strings. **First playable membrane / non-linear modal synth in 280+ prototypes.** The pitches are Bessel eigenmodes (inharmonic, non-pentatonic by physics); the tension pitch-glide is the non-linear part of the research anchor.
- **(3) Named reference** ‚Äî README cites **nlm (arXiv 2603.10240, 2026)** + **Rayleigh, *Theory of Sound* (1877)** + **Morrison & Rossing (tympani nonlinearity)**.
- **(2) ‚â•3 subsystems** ‚Äî 4: multi-touch strike (position + pseudo-velocity) input ¬∑ per-strike Bessel-mode displacement on the three.js mesh (viz) ¬∑ 8-partial non-linear modal audio synth bank with per-mode decay + tension detune ¬∑ three.js render (lights, glow rings, always-on drone).
- **(5) Recent research** ‚Äî built directly off this cycle's ¬ß292 dive.

**Diversity audit** (last 10 committed by folder = 264,267,268,271,272,275,276,279,280,283):
`diversity: banned=[canvas2d-output(5√ó: 271,272,275,279,280) ¬∑ C-major-pentatonic(JURY)] ¬∑ picked=[touch-strike INPUT ¬∑ three.js OUTPUT ¬∑ non-linear modal membrane synthesis TECHNIQUE(0√ó ever) ¬∑ matte/organic kids VIBE ¬∑ JI-spread Bessel-eigenmode SOUND (non-pentatonic)]`
- **OUTPUT = three.js**: canvas2d hit **5√ó** (271,272,275,279,280) ‚Üí **BANNED**; three.js only 2√ó (267,283), SVG 2√ó (268,276), raw-WebGL 1√ó (264) ‚Äî all under the line. All three briefs avoided canvas2d (284/285 three.js, 286 SVG).
- **INPUT**: no modality ‚â•4 (touch 3√ó [264,268,272], mic 3√ó [275,280,283], audio-file ~2√ó, tilt 1√ó, external-API 1√ó) ‚Üí touch is *under* the ban line at 3; chosen as the core 4-yo modality (last two kids inputs were tilt `276` + mic `280`, so touch rotates back in cleanly). The real diversification this cycle is the **sound engine**, which is the axis the JURY says nobody audits.
- **TECHNIQUE**: physical-modeling / modal synthesis = **0√ó ever** ‚Üí cleanest possible dodge.
- **VIBE**: kids 5√ó (264,268,272,276,280) ‚Üí forced (even cycle); diversified *inside* the kids zone via a wholly new synthesis method + new visual paradigm (membrane ripple).
- **SOUND** (the JURY's demanded axis): C-pentatonic ‚Üí **banned**; all three briefs forced non-pentatonic (Bessel eigenmodes / inharmonic bell partials / just intonation). Winner's pitches are physical eigenmodes ‚Äî the deepest break.

**Orchestration**: spawned 3 Builder subagents in parallel (single message), folder-isolated (284/285/286 pre-allocated), each fully self-contained (per-object concept + algorithm; audio-visual-only / self-contained / degrade-gracefully constraints; the per-brief mandatory non-canvas2d output; KIDS design rules; typography rules; the exact GitHub design-notes URL; the build-trap list incl. unused-vars, `use*`-named-helper hook trap, un-disabled `any`, the `Uint8Array<ArrayBuffer>` getByte* cast trap, refs-not-state rAF, one-`useEffect` teardown; and the no-git / no-shared-docs limits). All three returned demoable + README, each self-reporting tsc+eslint clean (286's builder fixed an engine `useEffect` that depended on `[started,hint]` and tore down the whole audio graph when the hint faded ‚Äî removed `hint` from deps; 285's builder noted a transient ENOENT during page-data that cleared on the clean re-run). I (Curator) gotcha-scanned all three (`"use client"` line 1 ¬∑ **no `getContext("2d")`** anywhere ¬∑ 284/285 `three`, 286 inline-SVG ¬∑ no `function use*` non-hook helpers ¬∑ no bare `any` ¬∑ each design-notes link points at its OWN README ¬∑ no API route), then ran the authoritative `npm run build` with **all three present** ‚Üí **exit 0** (honest banking: both losers genuinely compile + emit their routes ‚Äî 285 `3.89 kB`), then picked the winner, `rm -rf`'d the two losers, and rebuilt winner-only ‚Üí **"‚úì Compiled successfully in 27.8s"**, `284-kids-thunder-drum ‚óã Static 3.69 kB`.

**Winner = `284-kids-thunder-drum`.** All three cleared the floor and dodged every banned tag; decider = **ambition √ó surprise √ó demo-reliability √ó directive-fit**:
- **Most demo-reliable at 06:30** ‚Äî a satisfying boom on the *first tap* with zero gesture to learn. (285's signature *requires discovering* the circular-rub ‚Äî a plain tap gives only a small strike ‚Äî the same "wow needs a learned gesture/setup" risk that lost siblings in 282/289.)
- **Most novel / surprising SOUND + deepest JURY answer** ‚Äî the only one using the *non-linear* half of the research anchor: the tension pitch-glide "bwooOOWww" is the cleanest "huh, I didn't know we could do that," and eigenmodes-not-a-scale is the deepest possible answer to "audit the sound."
- **Most visceral for a 4-yo** ‚Äî drumming is the most primal kid instrument; three.js membrane-ripple ties the visual directly to the modal sound.
- Both runners-up **build-verified** (all-three build, exit 0) and banked number-free in IDEAS.md (`kids-singing-bowl` friction/stick-slip + `kids-jelly-choir` mass-spring soft-body).

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First playable physical-modeling / non-linear modal-synthesis instrument in 280+ prototypes** ‚Äî the pitches are circular-membrane Bessel **eigenmodes** (inharmonic, non-pentatonic by physics), not a chosen scale.
2. **First tension pitch-glide** ‚Äî strike velocity bends all partials sharp then relaxes them over 120‚Äì260 ms (the tympani/thunder-drum "bwooOOWww"); the non-linear part of the ¬ß292 anchor.
3. **Strike position = timbre** ‚Äî center hits excite low axisymmetric modes (round boom), rim hits the higher modes (bright slap), mirrored by a travelling rim-pinned membrane ripple that displaces the three.js mesh.
4. **Direct research‚Üíbuild chain, same fire** ‚Äî ¬ß292's physical-modeling dive IS the winner's load-bearing idea, and it answers the JURY "audit the SOUND" mandate at the synthesis-method level (deeper than the recent tuning-only breaks at 272/276/280).

**Queued next**:
- Cycle 293: **adult build** (293 % 2 = 1). Resurrect a banked non-winner now that canvas2d should start to unban: **`281-midi-harmonograph`** (harmonograph + JI, pianist fit, raw-WebGL2 ‚Äî canvas-free, shippable any cycle), **`282-ensemble-tabs`** (first networked piece), or **`mosaic-listener`** (first concatenative synthesis, canvas2d ‚Äî wait for unban). OR the JURY's standing ask: **AI-image-INSIDE-an-AV-piece** (his stated #1, still 0√ó in 15) or **extend `259-paths-generative`** (long-form/memory). Avoid a 4th "fly through your music" + 5th emergent-CA (JURY-frozen).
- Cycle 294: **kids build**. Bank-resurrect a fresh physical-modeling sibling ‚Äî **`kids-singing-bowl`** (friction/stick-slip, three.js) or **`kids-jelly-choir`** (mass-spring soft-body, SVG) ‚Äî both build-verified this fire; or push physical modeling further (a **plate/Chladni *playable* instrument**, or a struck **metal tongue-drum**). Keep diversifying the SOUND; rotate input/output against whatever the audit bans that cycle.
- **DEEPEN 284 (multi-cycle)**: coupled-mode solver (energy exchange between partials ‚Üí truer transient); amplitude-dependent damping; a tympani-style "pitch pedal" drag-to-retune gesture; a shell/air-cavity resonance under the membrane. README lists the path.
- **Housekeeping (open since 269)**: STATE.md ordering is consistent at the top now (286/287/288/291/292 prepended) but cycles 280‚Äì285 + 289/290 still sit appended at the END; INDEX.md "Newest/Previous" chain skips some mid-200s + 279/280. Worth a reconcile in a polish cycle.

**Notes**:
- **No API route / no `guard` needed** ‚Äî `284` is Web Audio + three.js, entirely client-side; no network, no mic, no camera, no server, no secrets. Within the scope fence (`src/app/dream/**` + `docs/dreams/**`). No new deps (three.js ^0.182 already installed).
- **WIDE-curation discipline held** ‚Äî built **all 3 present first** (exit 0 ‚Äî confirms both losers genuinely `‚óã Static` + emit routes, so banking is honest) then winner-only (exit 0). Three folder-isolated builders wrote concurrently with zero conflict; none touched git or shared docs; I (orchestrator) alone committed. Loser folders 285/286 `rm -rf`'d, never committed ‚Üí free again; committed folder this cycle = **284** only.
- **Diversity audit was load-bearing** ‚Äî it forced all three outputs off the over-represented canvas2d; the kids zone's monotony is the *sound*, so the WIDE fire put three never-used synthesis methods in play and shipped the one whose sound is most novel.
- **Honest carry-forward**: `284` is build-verified but not *browser-verified* live this fire. Risks (in README): (a) the per-strike modal voice is a sine bank, not a coupled-mode solver, so the attack transient is approximate (still reads as a struck skin); (b) "velocity" is derived from inter-tap timing / drag, not real pressure ‚Äî a single deliberate tap reads as medium-hard, so the glide is always *present* but its dynamic range is coarse. Both fail safe: the always-on drone keeps it alive, and any tap produces an immediate satisfying boom.

Both fail safe: the always-on synth pad keeps the form alive regardless of source.

**Research-first dive** (mandate; RESEARCH.md ¬ß288, 2026-06-02 ‚Äî web tools healthy): **non-octave tuning ‚Äî the Bohlen‚ÄìPierce scale**. Sources: Xenharmonic Wiki + Elaine Walker/ZIA + Wikipedia (BP independently discovered by **Heinz Bohlen 1972** and **Max Mathews & John R. Pierce**, Bell Labs ~1984); freshness anchor that it's a *live* scene not a museum piece: *"A huge online gallery of microtonal music to listen to"* (The Microtone Fox, **Medium, May 2026**) + Elaine Walker's forthcoming BP album; timbre cross-ref = the active 2025‚Äì2026 interactive-resonator literature. Surprise/hook: every pitched piece in 280+ prototypes ‚Äî pentatonic, slendro, Hindustani JI, whole-tone ‚Äî still repeats at the **2:1 octave**; BP throws the octave out, repeating at the **tritave (3:1)** in **13 equal steps** (`3^(1/13)‚âà1.0882`, ~146¬¢), engineered around odd-harmonic chords (the **3:5:7 triad**, clarinet affinity). It sounds in-tune yet alien ‚Äî the purest "huh, I didn't know we could do that" sound the lab can offer, and a 4-year-old has no theory to unlearn. Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct: ¬ß288 ‚Üí WIDE brief A ‚Üí shipped `276-kids-balloon-tritave` (research-first rule path (a)).

**Decided**: Kids cycle (288 % 2 = 0). No blocker, nothing in-progress. The standing JURY (2026-06-02) demand is *"audit the SOUND ‚Äî the lab's most monotonous axis; force a non-pentatonic mode."* Recent kids fires already broadened the sound (slendro `268`, JI-beating `272`, banked raga/polyrhythm). **Deliberately did NOT ship the queued banked `274-kids-clay-clock`** ‚Äî the live diversity audit (below) bans both **touch-input** AND **canvas2d-output** this cycle, and clay-clock is touch + canvas2d. The mandate says the diversity audit is a hard gate and "stop resurrecting the path of least resistance," so I picked a fresh direction that dodges the banned tags instead. (Path (a): build implements today's research hook.)

**Mode**: **WIDE** (alternation: 286 WIDE ‚Üí 287 DEEP ‚Üí **288 WIDE**; and JURY's "audit/diversify the sound" = go WIDE with fresh tags. Also: never solo kids ‚Üí orchestrate). Three briefs = three unrelated non-pentatonic kids SOUND worlds, each with a *different* non-banned input AND output:
- `276-kids-balloon-tritave` ‚Äî **TILT** + **SVG** + **Bohlen‚ÄìPierce non-octave tuning** (the novel-technique reading).
- `277-kids-overtone-cave` ‚Äî **MIC** + **raw-WebGL** + **harmonic-series overtone singing** (khoomei/Lucier reading).
- `278-kids-dream-flock` ‚Äî **CAMERA frame-diff** + **SVG** + **whole-tone / Debussy** boids flock (the dream-cloud reading).

- **(1) Novel technique** ‚Äî grepped INDEX + all READMEs for `bohlen`/`tritave`/`non-octave`: **zero hits ‚Üí first non-octave tuning in 280+ prototypes.** Every prior pitched piece (incl. slendro `268`, JI `272`, whole-tone shepard-tones) repeats at the 2:1 octave; BP repeats at the 3:1 tritave. (Note: modal/Karplus synthesis and MediaPipe/WebGPU/breath were all checked and are already used ‚Äî so the novelty is specifically the *tuning system*, not the synth/sensor.)
- **(3) Named reference** ‚Äî README cites the **Bohlen‚ÄìPierce scale**: Heinz Bohlen (1972) + Max Mathews & John R. Pierce (Bell Labs ~1984) + Elaine Walker (ZIA), with the clarinet/odd-harmonic affinity.
- **(2) ‚â•3 subsystems** ‚Äî 4 distinct: `DeviceOrientation` tilt input + gravity-drift physics (with mouse fallback) ¬∑ inline-SVG cut-paper render/animation engine (rAF via refs, feDropShadow + feTurbulence) ¬∑ odd-harmonic clarinet-like BP additive synth + root/tritave drone ¬∑ frame-based creature‚Üîballoon brush-collision (chords on simultaneous brush).
- **(5) Recent research** ‚Äî built directly off this cycle's ¬ß288 dive.

`diversity: banned=[touch-input(4√ó: 260,264,268,272) ¬∑ canvas2d-output(4√ó: 259,271,272,275) ¬∑ three.js-additive-points(JURY) ¬∑ C-major-pentatonic(JURY)] ¬∑ picked=[tilt input ¬∑ SVG output ¬∑ Bohlen‚ÄìPierce non-octave tuning technique ¬∑ matte cut-paper kids vibe ¬∑ non-pentatonic/non-octave SOUND]`
- INPUT: touch √ó4 (260,264,268,272) ‚Üí **BANNED**; mic √ó2 (271,275), camera √ó1 (258), audio-file √ó3 (259,267,+271), external-API √ó1 (262), tilt **0√ó** in last 10. Picked **tilt** (cleanest dodge; last tilt was 253 at #11).

I (Curator) gotcha-scanned all three (`"use client"` first line; **no three.js import** anywhere; no `function use*` non-hook helpers; no bare `any`; each design-notes link points at its OWN README), then ran the authoritative `npm run build` with **all three present** ‚Üí exit 0, "‚úì Compiled successfully in 27.7s", all three `‚óã Static` (276 4.07 kB ¬∑ 277 4.43 kB ¬∑ 278 4.2 kB) ‚Üí all three genuinely **build-verified** ‚Äî then picked the winner, `rm -rf`'d the two losers, and rebuilt winner-only (exit 0, "‚úì Compiled successfully in 29.3s", `276-kids-balloon-tritave` `‚óã Static` **4.07 kB**).

**Winner = `276-kids-balloon-tritave`.** All three cleared the floor and dodged every banned tag; the decider was **ambition √ó surprise √ó demo-reliability**:
- **Highest ambition (4/5) ‚Äî the only one clearing *novel-technique*.** First non-octave tuning in 280+ prototypes; it answers the JURY "audit the sound" mandate at the deepest possible level (it changes the *repeat interval itself*, not merely the scale). 277 (3/5) and 278 (2‚Äì3/5) are fresh SOUNDS but additive synthesis / whole-tone already exist in the lab.

- Both runners-up **build-verified** and banked number-free in IDEAS.md (`277-kids-overtone-cave` + `278-kids-dream-flock`).

**What's new about this prototype**:

4. **Direct research‚Üíbuild chain, same fire** ‚Äî ¬ß288's non-octave-tuning dive IS the winner's load-bearing idea.

## Cycle 287 ‚Äî adult build (DEEP orchestration) ‚Äî 275-memory-loom

**Research-first dive** (mandate; RESEARCH.md ¬ß287, 2026-06-02): the **self-listening musical-agent frontier** ‚Äî MACAT/MACataRT (arXiv **2502.00023**, Jan 2025: self-listening + audio mosaicing), Diemo Schwarz's **CataRT** (corpus-based concatenative synthesis), *The Concatenator* (2411.04366), with verified-fresh last-30/60-day anchors *Towards Real-Time Human‚ÄìAI Musical Co-Performance* (arXiv **2604.07612**, Apr 2026) and *Live Music Models*/Magenta-RT (2508.04651). Surprise/hook: the heavyweight 2026 frontier (latent-diffusion co-performance) needs a remote GPU and isn't browser-feasible, but the **memory architecture** under it runs zero-dep in Web Audio ‚Äî and the cheapest version is the most personal: the instrument becomes literally *made of the player's own sound*. The literature offers three memory representations (verbatim audio / grain corpus / symbolic notes) ‚Üí became this cycle's DEEP fan-out. Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct: ¬ß287 ‚Üí DEEP brief A ‚Üí shipped `275-memory-loom` (research-first rule path (a)).

**Decided**: Adult cycle (287 % 2 = 1). No blocker, nothing in-progress. The JURY's (2026-06-02) two "massively bigger" adult asks were: (#1) build the AI-image-INSIDE-AV piece (answered cycle 285 by `271-pigment-mosaic`), and (the freshest) **"Extend 259, not 243 ‚Äî fuse 259's incommensurate-loop state machine with his real Welcome Home recordings and the mic-listening intelligence proven in 251/256: a piece that *listens, remembers, and rewrites its own structure over 10 minutes.*"** Picked the latter ‚Äî 259 is the JURY's named standout / "most important build in the window," and a piece that *listens & remembers* is brand-new (259 only played back).

**Mode**: **DEEP** (alternation: 285 DEEP ‚Üí 286 WIDE ‚Üí **287 DEEP**; and the JURY's "extend X / massively bigger" signal = go DEEP on X). One massive concept ‚Äî *"a piece that LISTENS to you play, REMEMBERS what you played, and REWRITES its own structure over 10 min"* ‚Äî attacked via **three different memory representations**:
- `275-memory-loom` ‚Äî **verbatim audio capture** ‚Üí incommensurate loop bank + state machine (Frippertronics/Eno).
- `276-mosaic-listener` ‚Äî **grain corpus** tagged by descriptors ‚Üí concatenative navigation (CataRT/MACataRT).
- `277-motif-memory` ‚Äî **symbolic note transcription** ‚Üí Markov + transformational re-performance (Lewis *Voyager* / Rowe *Cypher*).

**Ambition floor** ‚Äî winner cleared **4 of 5** (need 2): `ambition: novel-technique + named-references + ‚â•3-subsystems + recent-research`
- **(1) Novel technique** ‚Äî grepped INDEX + READMEs: loops existed (`172`) and granular (`227`), but **live audio-capture-into-the-loop-bank (recording you in real time) / a self-listening Frippertronics architecture has never appeared** in 270+ prototypes. First of its kind.
- **(3) Named references** ‚Äî README cites **Eno & Fripp** *Frippertronics* / *(No Pussyfooting)* (1973) + *Music for Airports* (1978) and **Pauline Oliveros** *Deep Listening*.
- **(2) ‚â•3 subsystems** ‚Äî 4 distinct: mic-capture listener (ring buffer + 2-threshold RMS onset/release phrase slicer) ¬∑ incommensurate 7-loop memory bank (per-voice LFO + transposition + pan + procedural convolution reverb + shared lowpass + limiter) ¬∑ long-form generative movement state machine (decaying-memory displacement) ¬∑ Canvas2D loom viz. Plus tri-modal source (mic/file/`/api/audio` track-id/synth).
- **(5) Recent research** ‚Äî built directly off this cycle's ¬ß287 dive.

**Diversity audit** (last 10 by cycle = 272,271,268,267,264,262,260,259,258,256):
`diversity: banned=[touch-input(4√ó: 272,268,264,260) ¬∑ kids-vibe(5√ó: 272,268,264,260,258)] ¬∑ picked=[mic + audio-file input ¬∑ canvas2d output ¬∑ live-capture-looping + generative-state-machine technique ¬∑ ambient/long-form adult-meditative vibe]`
- INPUT: touch √ó4 ‚Üí **BANNED** (winner uses mic + audio-file, both under the line). OUTPUT: canvas2d √ó3 (272,271,259), three.js √ó2, raw-WebGL √ó2 ‚Äî none ‚â•4, canvas2d chosen (continues rebalancing off the JURY-flagged three.js glut). TECHNIQUE: FFT-spectral √ó2, kids-CA √ó2 ‚Äî none ‚â•4. VIBE: kids √ó5 ‚Üí **BANNED** (adult cycle avoids cleanly); cosmic √ó3.
- All three briefs were adult / mic+audio-file / canvas2d ‚Üí dodged both banned tags cleanly. The winner is also the most JURY-aligned (it literally *is* the "extend 259 ‚Üí listens, remembers, rewrites over 10 min" ask).

**Orchestration**: spawned 3 Builder subagents in parallel (single message), folder-isolated (275/276/277 pre-allocated), each fully briefed with constraints + typography rules + Canvas2D + the exact `/api/audio/:id` fetch pattern + the exact GitHub README URL + no-git/no-shared-docs limits. All three returned demoable + README, each self-reporting tsc+eslint clean. I (Curator) gotcha-scanned all three (no three.js/WebGL/additive-blend ‚Äî 275's only "three" match was a code comment; no `use*` helpers; `"use client"` first line; correct GitHub README link; `as any` properly eslint-disabled in 277; no API route created), then ran the authoritative `npm run build` with **all three present** ‚Üí exit 0, "‚úì Compiled successfully", all three `‚óã Static` (275 5.51 kB ¬∑ 276 5.73 kB ¬∑ 277 6.62 kB) ‚Üí all three genuinely **build-verified** ‚Äî then picked the winner, `rm -rf`'d the two losers, and rebuilt winner-only (exit 0, "‚úì Compiled in 35.8s", `275-memory-loom` `‚óã Static` **5.51 kB**).

**Winner = `275-memory-loom`.** Reasoning: (1) **Bullseye of the JURY's #1 adult ask** ‚Äî it reuses 259's *exact* incommensurate-loop engine and adds the mic-listening capture, so it IS "fuse 259 + listen + remember + rewrite over 10 min," almost verbatim. (2) **Strongest love + directive alignment** ‚Äî by keeping the *raw audio*, the room is literally built from Karel's own piano sound, sitting dead-center on the loved 163/227/172/259 cluster and his standing "use the real recordings" directive (the other two abstract the sound into grains/symbols). (3) **Demo-reliable** ‚Äî no fragile pitch detection (277's monophonic-detection risk; 276 is also robust but more abstract); the D-dorian pad means it's never silent even before you play. (4) **Novel** ‚Äî first live-capture / self-listening Frippertronics architecture in the lab. 276 (first concatenative synthesis ‚Äî highest *pure* novelty) and 277 (symbolic *Voyager* partner) banked build-verified in IDEAS.md.

**What I built/shipped**:
- `src/app/dream/275-memory-loom/page.tsx` ‚Äî a self-listening Frippertronics room. A muted mic tap ‚Üí `ScriptProcessor`(4096) ‚Üí smoothed RMS ‚Üí 8 s rolling **ring buffer**; a two-threshold onset/release detector marks phrase boundaries (4.5 s cap, 0.6 s floor) and on phrase-end slices the recent audio out of the ring, peak-normalizes, end-fades for loopability, and banks it. Up to **7 verbatim phrase loops**, each a looping `AudioBufferSourceNode` with consonant `playbackRate` transposition (octave/5th/4th/M3/unison), stereo pan, and an incommensurate gain-LFO (0.041‚Äì0.127 Hz); a separate incommensurate playhead rate (0.131‚Äì0.257) drives the visual sweep; full bank ‚Üí **oldest memory decays out** (demo seeds first, then by capture time). A generative **movement state machine** every 60‚Äì130 s random-walks the active set (3‚Äì5 voices, 2.5 s in / 4.5 s out crossfades via `setTargetAtTime`), transpositions, density, lowpass cutoff (900‚Äì4100 Hz), and reverb wet/dry. Signal chain: per-voice ‚Üí shared lowpass ‚Üí dry + procedural-IR convolution reverb wet ‚Üí `DynamicsCompressor` limiter ‚Üí destination (never clips/silent). Tri-modal source: **mic** ¬∑ **file-drop** ¬∑ **Welcome Home track-id** ‚Üí read-only `fetch('/api/audio/:id')` (JSON-`{url}`-or-raw-bytes) ¬∑ always-on **D-dorian** synth demo pad (non-pentatonic). Canvas2D **loom**: per-phrase lanes (waveform + incommensurate playhead + gain-brightness + warm/amber hue + `captured Nm ago` label), capture flash, live input RMS meter, HUD (elapsed / movement # / active-total voices / source). Full teardown (sources stopped, rAF cancelled, mic tracks stopped, `audioCtx.close()`). Typography rules followed.
- `src/app/dream/275-memory-loom/README.md` ‚Äî concept; what's novel (keeps raw audio, not features); 4 subsystems; signal-chain diagram; state-machine table; degradation table; named refs (Eno/Fripp + Oliveros); honest limits (deprecated `ScriptProcessor` main-thread capture, RMS-only onset, naive normalization, `playbackRate` couples pitch+duration, sources not monitored dry); next-cycle deepening (AudioWorklet + spectral-flux onset, pitch-preserving transposition, regenerative feedback-delay tape path, persistent/exportable memory, steerable movements).
- Banked both build-verified non-winners as seeds in IDEAS.md and `rm -rf`'d their folders (never committed, per orchestration safety): **`mosaic-listener`** (CataRT/MACataRT grain-corpus concatenative synthesis ‚Äî the lab's **first concatenative synthesis**, the strongest single novelty bank; queued for the next clean-slate adult cycle) and **`motif-memory`** (autocorrelation pitch-transcription ‚Üí symbolic Markov/transformational re-performance, the Lewis *Voyager* / Rowe *Cypher* lineage ‚Äî most musically-literate, extends loved `256`). Appended RESEARCH ¬ß287. Updated INDEX Newest. Rewrote MORNING.md.

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First time the lab records YOU in real time and plays you back as the instrument.** Not a visualizer, not file playback ‚Äî a live self-listening loop. The instrument becomes made of your own sound.
2. **The most literal extension of loved 259 + Karel's "use his real recordings" directive** ‚Äî same incommensurate-loop engine, but it *listens* and *remembers*, and keeps the raw piano sound.
3. **Brings the self-listening-agent architecture (MACAT/CataRT) into the lab via its cheapest, most personal form** ‚Äî zero deps, zero latency, browser-native, where the 2026 GPU frontier can't reach.
4. **DEEP shipped the most JURY-aligned of three memory representations**, banking the lab's first concatenative-synthesis piece and a symbolic *Voyager* partner ‚Äî two more fresh techniques queued.

**What I built/shipped**:
- `src/app/dream/272-kids-tune-purr/page.tsx` ‚Äî a warm **110 Hz drone** + **3 matte "hummer" creatures** (116 px drag targets) on a deep-dusk matte canvas. Each creature starts **5.5‚Äì7 Hz detuned** from a pure JI target against the drone (unison 1:1, major third 5:4, perfect fifth 3:2) ‚Üí genuine **acoustic beating** you hear *and see* (the creature shivers at the beat rate, reinforced by a beat-frequency LFO AM). Dragging Y changes pitch continuously; the beat slows as you near the ratio; within **¬±6 cents** it **snaps into tune** ‚Äî shivering stops, eyes open, smile, a soft **purr** fades in. Lock all 3 ‚Üí a pure **4:5:6 just-intonation major triad** + a matte ring-wave celebration. Fully reversible, no score, no fail. Pointer Events + `setPointerCapture` + `touch-none`; AudioContext unlocks on first gesture; all gains via `setTargetAtTime` (click-free, toddler-safe, nothing above 350 Hz); full rAF + `AudioContext.close()` teardown. **No three.js/WebGL, no additive blending ‚Äî true drop-shadows only.**
- `src/app/dream/272-kids-tune-purr/README.md` ‚Äî concept; JI ratio + beat-math table (4:5:6); sound design; visual-state map; degradation; named refs (Helmholtz + McBride 2510.14159 + Parncutt & Hair); honest limits (LFO-AM phase drift, ¬±170-cent drag clamp, monophonic purr); next-cycle deepening (haptics on lock, ring-mod for phase-coherent beats, 4th creature on the 4:3, visual interference-waveform trace).
- Banked both build-verified non-winners as seeds in IDEAS.md and `rm -rf`'d their folders (never committed, per orchestration safety): **`273-kids-raga-peacock`** (tanpura drone + Raga Yaman just-intonation + meend glide, SVG cut-paper ‚Äî *deliberately deferred*: its non-Western-tuning + matte-cut-paper aesthetic sits too close to last kids cycle's `268-kids-shadow-theater`; bank for spacing) and **`274-kids-clay-clock`** (first true cross-rhythm/polyrhythm kids toy: 2:3:4 + 12/8 gankogui bell on a Chris-Wilson two-clock look-ahead scheduler, matte clay, percussion-only). Appended RESEARCH ¬ß286. Updated INDEX Newest. Rewrote MORNING.md. Added a KIDS.md "what's been built" row.

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First time audible acoustic beating is the instrument.** Not a visualizer of sound ‚Äî the child *resolves roughness into consonance* with their hand, by ear. The lab's first piece about tuning/intonation itself.
2. **Answers the JURY's two un-audited over-used axes at once** ‚Äî non-luminous (matte) AND non-pentatonic (pure JI ratios over a drone), the two tags the rolling-10 audit kept missing.
3. **Brings real psychoacoustics into the kids zone** ‚Äî the roughness‚Üíconsonance three-term model (Helmholtz ‚Üí McBride 2025) made playable for a 4-year-old.
4. **WIDE shipped the most JURY-aligned of three fresh non-pentatonic sound worlds**, banking a Hindustani-raga piece and a West-African-polyrhythm piece both build-verified ‚Äî two more brand-new kids sound palettes queued.

**What I built/shipped**:
- `src/app/dream/259-paths-generative/page.tsx` ‚Äî Eno/Reich generative ambient room: **6 incommensurate buffer loops** (11.3/14.7/18.1/23.9/29.3/37.7 s; product ‚âà 29 h before exact repeat) each on a slow per-voice LFO-gain (0.023‚Äì0.071 Hz, incommensurate) + consonant transposition (octave/5th/4th/M3 via `playbackRate`) + stereo pan, summed through a synthesized-noise **convolution reverb** + shared lowpass. A **generative state machine** fires a new "movement" every 90‚Äì150 s (√∑ drift slider) that random-walks the active-voice subset (3‚Äì5 of 6, 6-s fades), filter cutoff (200‚Äì4000 Hz), reverb wet (0.2‚Äì0.9), transposition set, palette, and density, keeping movement memory so it evolves (minute 1 ‚âÝ 5 ‚âÝ 10). Tri-modal source: file-drop ¬∑ "load Resonance track by ID" ‚Üí `/api/audio/:id` (handles direct-audio *and* JSON `{url}`, the loved-`163` pattern, so Karel can paste a real Welcome Home track) ¬∑ synthesized demo fallback. Canvas-2D: 6 concentric rings rotating at `1/loopLength` rad/s (you *see* the phase drift) with per-voice glow + playhead + a movement-tinted radial field. Readout: elapsed / movement # / active voices. Full audio-graph + rAF teardown on unmount. Typography rules followed.
- `src/app/dream/259-paths-generative/README.md` ‚Äî concept; loop table (length/rate/pan/LFO) + WHY; signal chain; state-machine spec; source priority; visual mapping; degradation table; named refs (Eno + Reich); honest limitations (loop-boundary clicks, simple demo, `playbackRate` couples pitch+rhythm, 60 fps `setElapsedDisplay` React churn); next-cycle deepening (granular re-synth, key-aware tuning, localStorage persistence, WAV export).
- Banked both build-verified non-winners as seeds in IDEAS.md and `rm -rf`'d their folders (never committed, per orchestration safety): **`260-aurora-drone`** (live NOAA solar-wind ‚Üí drone+aurora ‚Äî the JURY-requested **2nd real-world-data sonification** after `233`; build-verified, 705 lines, clean) and **`261-live-duet-groover`** (mic ‚Üí spectral-flux/two-clocks generative drums ‚Äî the **rhythm member that completes the AI-band trio** melody-251/harmony-256; build-verified, 1081 lines, clean). Appended RESEARCH ¬ß279. Added INDEX Newest section. Rewrote MORNING.md.

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First long-form (>5 min) stateful generative piece in the lab.** Loops (`172`) and granular (`227`) existed; a piece with movements + memory that's *different at minute 10* did not. It's the "leave it running" category the diversity menu flagged as empty.

3. **Eno's 1978 trick beats a 2026 diffusion model for this job.** The research dive's payoff: incommensurate loops + a slow state machine get genuinely non-repeating long-form with zero deps and zero latency ‚Äî documented as the deliberate choice over latent-diffusion long-form gen.

## Cycle 277 ‚Äî adult build (DEEP orchestration) ‚Äî 256-live-duet-harmonist

**Git sync**: Fast-forwarded main to a9d779f (cycle 276, `253-kids-tilt-pour`; +38 commits). Worked directly on main. `node_modules` was ABSENT ‚Üí ran `npm ci` (exit 0) before the authoritative builds.

**Love signal** (32 loved; unchanged set this fire): the pull is the **chord/responsive-duet** cluster ‚Äî `251-live-duet-trader` ‚ù§Ô∏è (the melodic sibling, loved last review) + `229-chord-canvas` ‚ù§Ô∏è (loved chroma‚Üíchord naming) + the broad orchestration-era love wave (`243`/`236`/`234` ‚ù§Ô∏è). Karel loving 251 directly is the green light to ship its harmony counterpart ‚Äî the polyphonic half of the same "AI band."

**Research-first dive** (mandate; RESEARCH.md ¬ß277, 2026-06-02): **ReaLchords** (Lee et al., arXiv 2506.14723, **v1 17 Jun 2025**, date verified via WebFetch) ‚Äî an *online* model emitting chord accompaniment **simultaneously** with an unfolding monophonic melody, max-likelihood-pretrained then **RL-refined against a reward that scores harmonic AND temporal coherency separately** (+ teacher distillation from a future-aware model). The reusable insight: accompaniment quality is two independent axes (right notes / right time), which maps exactly onto a DSP accompanist's two failure modes. Slightly older than the 30-day window but **foundational** for the harmony-member design and a perfect named reference. Recent anchor remains arXiv 2604.07612 (Apr 2026) from the cycle-275 dive that seeded this whole family.

**Decided**: Adult cycle (277 % 2 = 1). No blocker, nothing in-progress. **This is a multi-cycle continuation** (research-first rule path (b)/(c)): the "AI band you play with" arc ‚Äî `251-live-duet-trader` shipped cycle 275; this cycle ships its **harmony member**, the banked build-verified `live-duet-harmonist`. Explicitly the queued-next from cycle 276 STATE and a JURY.md ask (extend the reactive-accompaniment thread, don't seed yet another idea).

**Ambition floor** ‚Äî winner cleared **4 of 5** (need 2): `ambition: recent-research-anchor + named-references + ‚â•3-subsystems + novel-technique`
- **(5) Recent research** ‚Äî built off the cycle-275 dive's arXiv 2604.07612 (Apr 2026) anchor that seeded the whole live-duet family + this cycle's ¬ß277 ReaLchords cite.
- **(3) Named references** ‚Äî README cites ReaLchords (2506.14723) + arXiv 2604.07612 + Chris Wilson *A Tale of Two Clocks* + Mark Levine *The Jazz Piano Book*.
- **(2) ‚â•3 subsystems** ‚Äî 4 distinct: chroma‚Üí36-template chord/quality detection ¬∑ jazz voicing engine (rootless/drop-2 + nearest-pitch voice-leading) + walking bass ¬∑ spectral-flux onset/tempo inference ¬∑ Chris-Wilson two-clocks look-ahead scheduler (+ Web Audio synth bed w/ feedback-delay + limiter).
- **(1) Novel technique** ‚Äî **first spectral-flux onset/tempo inference + two-clocks look-ahead scheduler in the lab** (the banked groover seed proposed beat-tracking but was never built; this is the first realization), and the first to fuse it *with* live harmony.

**Diversity audit** (last 10 = 234,236,238,243,244,246,248,251,253 + 233):
`diversity: banned=[three.js-output ¬∑ kids-vibe] ¬∑ picked=[mic input ¬∑ canvas2d output ¬∑ chroma‚Üíchord + jazz-voicing/onset-sync technique ¬∑ adult jazz/responsive vibe]`
- OUTPUT tallies: **three.js ‚â•6** (234,236,238,243,244,246) ‚Üí BANNED. raw-WebGL = 2 (248,253); canvas2d = 2 (233,251). VIBE: **kids = 5** (234,238,244,248,253) ‚Üí BANNED for an adult cycle anyway. INPUT: mic/voice ~4 (243,244,251 + this) ‚Äî note: mic is climbing; acceptable here because it's the necessary input for a live accompanist and the OUTPUT (canvas2d, under-represented) + technique are fresh.
- 256 uses **canvas2d** (under-represented, helps rebalance away from the three.js glut JURY flagged) + **mic** + a technique stack the lab has never shipped. Dodges both banned tags cleanly.

**Mode**: **DEEP** (alternation: 275 DEEP ‚Üí 276 WIDE ‚Üí **277 DEEP**). The target is a single named banked concept (the harmony member), so DEEP ‚Äî one concept, two technical approaches in parallel ‚Äî is correct over WIDE. Two briefs attacked the same "live comping harmonist" via different voicing/timing engines:
- `250-live-duet-harmonist` ‚Äî the canonical banked design: fixed-72-BPM eighth-note arpeggiated **triad** comping, nearest-octave voice leading, max demo-reliability.
- `256-live-duet-harmonist` ‚Äî a **jazz** variant: rootless/drop-2 voicings + walking bass + **onset-synced** comping on a two-clocks scheduler (folds the seed's own "next-cycle deepening" into the shipped piece).

**Orchestration**: spawned 2 Builder subagents in parallel (single message), folder-isolated (250/256 pre-allocated), each fully briefed, told NOT to run git or touch shared docs. Both returned demoable + README, each self-reporting eslint+tsc clean. I (Curator) ran the authoritative `npm run build` with **both present** ‚Üí exit 0, "‚úì Compiled successfully", both routes `‚óã Static` (250 4.23 kB ¬∑ 256 5.63 kB) ‚Äî so both are genuinely **build-verified** ‚Äî then picked the winner, `rm -rf`'d the loser, and rebuilt winner-only (exit 0, `256-live-duet-harmonist` `‚óã Static` **5.63 kB**).

**Winner = `256-live-duet-harmonist`.** Reasoning: (1) **Higher ambition floor** ‚Äî clears 4/5 vs 250's 3/5; it brings genuinely new DSP (spectral-flux onset/tempo + two-clocks scheduler) the lab has never had, fused with harmony. (2) **Bigger concept per the mandate** ("massively bigger concepts / bigger is fine") ‚Äî it folds the seed's *own* deepening list (rootless/drop-2 voicings, walking bass, onset-synced comping) into the shipped piece instead of leaving them for "next cycle"; the result reads as a jazz trio, not a pad. (3) **More surprise** ‚Äî "a comping bed that locks to the rhythm of your playing, not a metronome" is the line that makes Karel lean in. (4) **Degrades gracefully** ‚Äî the onset-sync risk (spectral flux is weak on legato piano ‚Äî the groover caveat) is contained: sparse onsets fall back to 80 BPM and the jazz voicings don't depend on tempo accuracy, and the demo path is a deterministic ii‚ÄìV‚ÄìI‚Äìvi at 88 BPM. The runner-up **`250` is banked build-verified** in IDEAS.md as `live-duet-harmonist-simple` ‚Äî the more demo-reliable fixed-clock version; resurrect/ship it as a "safe mode" if 256's tempo-sync proves flaky in a real browser session.

**Authoritative build**: winner-only `npm run build` ‚Üí exit 0, "‚úì Compiled successfully in 34.7s", `/dream/256-live-duet-harmonist` `‚óã Static` **5.63 kB**.

**What I built/shipped**:
- `src/app/dream/256-live-duet-harmonist/page.tsx` ‚Äî mic ‚Üí AnalyserNode(4096) ‚Üí 12-bin chroma (60‚Äì2000 Hz, one-pole 0.8) ‚Üí cosine-match 36 weighted templates (maj/min/dom7, shell-weighted) ‚Üí 160 ms settle latch ‚Üí **rootless/drop-2 jazz voicing engine** (3+7 shell + 9/13 color, nearest-pitch `setTargetAtTime` voice leading) + **walking bass** (root/5/3/chromatic-approach across the bar) ‚Üí **spectral-flux onsets** (adaptive `mean+1.5¬∑std`, 100 ms refractory) ‚Üí median-IOI tempo (folded 60‚Äì180, 80 BPM fallback) ‚Üí **Chris-Wilson two-clocks scheduler** (25 ms tick / 100 ms window, exact AudioContext times) placing bass + comp stabs on the inferred pulse. Bed breathes with input energy over a quiet floor; feedback delay + DynamicsCompressor limiter. Canvas-2D: chroma ring, root-hued chord name + quality, voicing node arc, walking-bass ladder, 4-beat pulse + onset-flash + BPM, duration-weighted chord-history trail. No-mic ‚Üí Dm7‚ÜíG7‚ÜíCmaj7‚ÜíAm7 @88 BPM with full bass+comp + `text-rose-300` notice + retry. Full rAF/scheduler/mic/AudioContext cleanup on unmount. Typography rules followed.
- `src/app/dream/256-live-duet-harmonist/README.md` ‚Äî concept; full pipeline with chosen params + WHY; voicing table per quality; color‚Üîroot table; degradation; named references (ReaLchords + 2604.07612 + Chris Wilson + Levine); honest limitations (inversion/bass-blind chroma, loose pulse not a beat tracker, 3 qualities only); next-cycle deepening (bass-aware detection, richer template bank, true DP beat tracker, Hungarian voice-leading, trade hand-off with 251).
- Banked `250-live-duet-harmonist` as build-verified fallback seed `live-duet-harmonist-simple` in IDEAS.md; `rm -rf`'d its folder (never committed, per orchestration safety). Marked the original `live-duet-harmonist` seed SHIPPED. Appended RESEARCH ¬ß277. Added INDEX Newest section. Rewrote MORNING.md.

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First reactive comping engine in the lab.** `229-chord-canvas` ‚ù§Ô∏è *names* your chords; this one *plays under them* with voiced harmony ‚Äî a living harmonic bed, not a label.
2. **First spectral-flux onset/tempo inference + two-clocks look-ahead scheduler**, and the first to drive harmony off it ‚Äî the bed locks to your pulse instead of a fixed clock.
3. **Completes the duet's polyphonic half.** 251 (melodic, monophonic) + 256 (chordal, polyphonic) now cover both how a pianist plays ‚Äî the "AI band" arc is two members deep (groover banked = the third).
4. **Orchestration shipped the more ambitious of two parallel approaches**, banking the safer one build-verified ‚Äî the critic chose ambition with a documented fallback, exactly the studio choreography Karel is building toward.

**Queued next**:
- Cycle 278: **kids build** (278 % 2 = 0). MUST be orchestrated (JURY: never solo ‚Äî regresses to the tap-chime) and dodge the still-banned three.js + touch+canvas2d+pentatonic kids template via non-touch input. Build-verified banked candidates: `254-kids-blow-bloom` (breath/blow input ‚Äî highest surprise, fresh) or `255-kids-sing-garden` (voice‚Üíbedtime sky ‚Äî hold until `244` breathes; swap in YIN/MPM first). Run WIDE for fresh tags or DEEP-resurrect a banked one.
- Cycle 279: **adult build** ‚Äî ship the **third band member** `live-duet-groover` (drums/tempo, build-verified, ~728 lines) so the trio is complete (harmony 256 + melody 251 + rhythm). Caveat: needs a real-browser session to tune its flux floor; same applies to 256's tempo-sync ‚Äî a one-off "tune the live-duet family by ear" polish cycle is worth scheduling once Karel can sit with them.
- **Housekeeping flag (open since 269)**: INDEX.md still missing detailed entries for 230‚Äì232, 236, 238, 243 ‚Äî worth a polish cycle to reconcile.

**Notes**:
- No API route / no `guard` needed: `getUserMedia` + Web Audio + Canvas only; nothing server-side, no secrets. Within the scope fence (`src/app/dream/**` + `docs/dreams/**`).
- DEEP-curation discipline held: built **both present** first (exit 0 ‚Äî confirms the banked loser genuinely compiles, so "build-verified" is honest) then winner-only. Two folder-isolated builders wrote concurrently with zero conflict; neither touched git or shared docs; I (orchestrator) alone committed. Pre-allocated 250/256 so no number collision (252 left reserved for the groover; 254/255 reserved as the kids seeds).
- Honest carry-forward: 256's tempo-sync is spectral-flux-based and will be weakest on legato piano (Karel's instrument) ‚Äî documented in the README, and 250 is banked as the fixed-clock safe-mode fallback if a browser session shows it's flaky.

The surprise isn't the metaball ‚Äî it's the *mobile-perf engineering recipe*: a **Hermite smoothstep** falloff instead of `exp()`, a **fixed 8-blob** unrolled loop, **2-octave noise cap**, and **DPR‚â§2** ("cuts GPU load in half on 3x phones with no perceptible quality loss") ‚Äî exactly what's needed to run a gooey-fluid look on a 4-year-old's tablet, in a kids zone that had **zero** metaball/smooth-min pieces. **Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct and visible: ¬ß276 dive ‚Üí WIDE brief A ‚Üí shipped `253-kids-tilt-pour`** (satisfies research-first rule path (a): implement the dive's hook).

CPU physics: `deviceorientation` gamma/beta ‚Üí smoothed gravity vector ‚Üí gravity + damping(0.985) + edge-restitution(0.55) + soft blob‚Äìblob repulsion, |v|‚â§0.5. Per-pair merge (dist<0.13, 260ms refractory) rings each blob's C-pentatonic note (sine+octave-triangle, 0.9s env) ‚Üí feedback delay ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor limiter; always-on low triad pad swells with the slosh metric. iOS `requestPermission()` behind the start tap (also unlocks audio); tilt-denied / no-events-in-1.8s ‚Üí pointer-drag-steers-gravity fallback + `text-rose-300` notice; no-WebGL ‚Üí notice + pad keeps playing. 3. **Direct research‚Üíbuild chain, same fire.** Today's Van Der Merwe dive named the exact mobile-perf recipe; the winner is its literal application on a kids tablet.
4. **Orchestration on the kids cadence, as JURY demanded.** Three unrelated non-touch directions explored in one fire; the critic shipped the most research-aligned + demo-reliable and banked the other two build-verified. No solo kids regression to the chime.

## Cycle 275 ‚Äî adult build (DEEP orchestration) ‚Äî 251-live-duet-trader

**When**: 2026-06-01 UTC (every-2h autonomous cycle)

**Git sync**: Fast-forwarded main to dcdbd68 (cycle 274, 248-kids-stir-garden). node_modules was ABSENT ‚Üí ran `npm ci` (clean, exit 0) for the authoritative build.

**Love signal** (32 loved): newly loved since cycle 274 ‚Äî `243-spectral-cloud` ‚ù§Ô∏è, `236-particle-life-song` ‚ù§Ô∏è, `234-kids-hand-creature` ‚ù§Ô∏è (the orchestrated floor-4+ builds are landing ‚Äî Karel is rewarding the ambition). `217-dance-avatar` ‚ù§Ô∏è also loved. The pattern: Karel loves the orchestration-era work and `225-aria-companion`-adjacent dialogue/responsive pieces (he loved `163`/`227` data‚Üíform, and the responsive-creature line). No single love forced the pick, but the broad love of responsive/dialogue + live-performance pieces supports the reactive-accompanist direction.

**Research-first dive** (mandate, ¬ß275 in RESEARCH.md, 2026-06-01): found **arXiv 2604.07612 ‚Äî "Towards Real-Time Human‚ÄìAI Musical Co-Performance: Accompaniment Generation with Latent Diffusion Models and MAX/MSP"** (08 Apr 2026, <60 days). Its key surprise is the *sliding-window look-ahead* co-performance protocol: the AI accompanist acts on **partial** context (predicts/settles ahead) with **latency as the binding constraint**, and a MAX/MSP‚ÜîPython split keeps the real-time front end decoupled from the heavy model. **Chain today's research ‚Üí today's build is direct: ¬ß275 ‚Üí the live-duet family ‚Üí `251-live-duet-trader`.**

**Decided**: Adult cycle (275 % 2 = 1). No blocker, nothing in-progress.

**Ambition floor** ‚Äî cleared **3 of 5** (need 2): `ambition: recent-research-cite + named-reference + 3-subsystems`
- **(5) Recent research finding (<14 days)** ‚Äî built directly off this cycle's RESEARCH ¬ß275 (arXiv 2604.07612, Apr 2026).
- **(3) Named reference** ‚Äî README cites arXiv 2604.07612 + Fran√ßois Pachet's "The Continuator" + the jazz practice of "trading fours."
- **(2) ‚â•3 subsystems** ‚Äî 4 distinct: mic capture + NSDF/McLeod autocorrelation pitch detection (onset/gap tracking) ¬∑ live-trained 1st-order Markov interval generator + key inference from a pitch-class histogram ¬∑ gap-triggered fill generator with duck-on-re-entry ¬∑ Web Audio synthesis (warm additive partner voice + feedback-delay + low pad) + Canvas-2D split piano-roll.
- (Brushes (1) novel-technique too: continuous **interleaved** trading with duck-on-re-entry is a genuinely new interaction in the lab ‚Äî `225-aria-companion` is fixed-2s turn-taking, not interleaved.)

**Diversity audit** (last 10 = 232,233,234,236,238,243,244,246,248 + 231):
`diversity: banned=[three.js/WebGL-output ¬∑ kids-vibe] ¬∑ picked=[mic-input ¬∑ canvas2d-output ¬∑ real-time-onset/gap+Markov-technique ¬∑ jazz/responsive-duet-vibe]`
- Tallies over the last 10: OUTPUT `three.js/WebGL` ‚â•7 (233,234,238,243,244,246,248) ‚Üí BANNED; VIBE `kids` 5 (232,234,238,244,248) ‚Üí BANNED. (INPUT touch 2, mic/voice ~3, canvas2d only 2 ‚Äî none ‚â•4.)
- Trader uses neither banned tag: OUTPUT = Canvas-2D (only its 3rd appearance in the window), VIBE = adult jazz/responsive, INPUT = mic (not over-represented). Clean. This is also an explicit JURY.md ask (provocation #4).

**Mode**: **DEEP** (JURY.md provocation: "fuse duet-shadow + 225-aria-companion into real reactive accompaniment"; the reactive-accompaniment thread is the named direction ‚Üí go DEEP on it, not WIDE on fresh tags). One massive concept ‚Äî **"a live AI accompanist that plays WITH you in real time"** ‚Äî attacked via THREE band-member approaches in parallel:
- `250-live-duet-harmonist` ‚Äî chroma‚Üíchord detection ‚Üí continuous bass + voice-led comp bed underneath (the bassist/pianist).
- `251-live-duet-trader` ‚Äî real-time gap detection ‚Üí live-trained Markov fills that interleave + duck on re-entry (the improviser).
- `252-live-duet-groover` ‚Äî spectral-flux onset ‚Üí tempo inference ‚Üí look-ahead two-clocks scheduled generative groove (the drummer).

**Orchestration**: spawned 3 Builder subagents in parallel (single message), each fully briefed and folder-isolated (250/251/252), told NOT to run git or touch shared docs. All three returned demoable + README, all self-verified lint+tsc clean.

**Winner = `251-live-duet-trader`.** Reasoning: (1) it's the build JURY.md asked for *by name* ‚Äî "the duet-shadow seed (mic onset ‚Üí generative answer) is sitting there‚Ä¶ 225-aria-companion already proved the dialogue UX. Fuse them: extend 225 into real reactive accompaniment." 251 is the literal realization. (2) Freshest *interaction*: continuous interleaved trading with duck-on-re-entry (you always have right of way) vs 225's fixed-2s turn-taking ‚Äî the biggest surprise for Karel. (3) Best "plays WITH me" narrative for a pianist playing a melodic line. The non-winners lost narrowly: `250-harmonist` overlaps `229-chord-canvas`'s chroma‚Üíchord detection (less novel) though it best handles *chordal* piano; `252-groover` is the most technically novel (first beat-tracker + two-clocks scheduler in the lab) but its builder flagged it's unverifiable by ear and spectral-flux tempo tracking is weakest on legato piano ‚Äî exactly Karel's instrument. Both banked build-verified in IDEAS.md as the next two band members (clear multi-cycle "AI band" arc: Trader ‚Üí Harmonist ‚Üí Groover).

**Authoritative build**: removed the two non-winner folders, ran `npm run build` with only 251 present ‚Üí exit 0, `/dream/251-live-duet-trader` `‚óã Static`, **5.48 kB**.

**What I built/shipped**:
- `src/app/dream/251-live-duet-trader/page.tsx` ‚Äî mic ‚Üí AnalyserNode (fftSize 2048) ‚Üí ~16ms NSDF/McLeod autocorrelation pitch detect (RMS-gated, 70ms debounce) ‚Üí onset stream ‚Üí live 1st-order Markov interval table + key inference (12-bin PC histogram ‚Üí best major fit ‚Üí major-pentatonic snap) ‚Üí **gap timer**: >320ms silence + ‚â•3 notes launches a key-constrained Markov fill on a warm additive partner voice (triangle + inharmonic partials + feedback-delay shimmer + low pad); **the instant a new onset is detected the partner ducks (50ms gain ramp + cancels its scheduled phrase)** ‚Äî you always have right of way. Canvas-2D split scrolling piano-roll (amber YOU / teal PARTNER, glow trails, LISTENING/ANSWERING badge, inferred-key label, filling gap-timer bar, live pitch dot). No-mic ‚Üí auto-running demo motif (plays, pauses, gets answered, loops) + `text-rose-300` notice. Full cleanup on unmount. New typography rules followed.
- `src/app/dream/251-live-duet-trader/README.md` ‚Äî concept, explicit contrast with 225-aria-companion (interleaved vs turn-based), pipeline, key inference, references, degradation, honest limitations, next-cycle ideas.
- Banked `250-live-duet-harmonist` + `252-live-duet-groover` as build-verified seeds in IDEAS.md; `rm -rf`'d both folders (never committed, per orchestration safety). Appended RESEARCH ¬ß275. Rewrote MORNING.md; added INDEX entry.

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First continuous interleaved trading in the lab.** Every reactive piece either responds continuously (mirror the signal) or takes fixed turns (`225`). Trading-fours-with-duck-on-re-entry is a third interaction model: the AI weaves *into the gaps* of your phrasing and gets out of the way the instant you re-enter. That conversational feel is the whole point.
2. **It realizes the jury's named request.** The reactive-accompaniment thread was the most-specifically-requested unbuilt direction; this closes it.
3. **Live-performance fitness (Karel's pillar #3).** Monophonic melodic input ‚Üí instant low-latency answer. Plays on a single melodic line with no setup; the demo shows it with no mic.
4. **Sets up a multi-cycle "AI band."** The two banked siblings (Harmonist = harmony, Groover = rhythm) are the next two members. Three reactive accompanists = a band you play with, not a toy.

**Queued next**:
- Cycle 276: **kids build** (276 % 2 = 0). MUST be orchestrated (JURY: never ship a solo kids build ‚Äî they regress to the tap-chime) and MUST clear the floor + dodge the still-banned three.js+kids combo via non-touch input. Strong one-cycle-ready candidates: the build-verified banked `kids-sing-garden` (voice‚Üíraw-GLSL bedtime sky ‚Äî fills the empty calm/pre-sleep niche) or `kids-tilt-pour` (tilt‚Üímetaball lava-lamp). Run WIDE if seeding fresh, or DEEP-resurrect a banked one.
- Cycle 277: **adult build** ‚Äî ship the **next band member**: `250-live-duet-harmonist` (build-verified, handles chordal piano ‚Äî the comping bed) so the live-duet concept exists as harmony + trading. Then `252-live-duet-groover` (rhythm) completes the trio. All three clear the floor (same research cite + named ref + ‚â•3 subsystems).

**Notes**:
- No API route / no `guard` needed: getUserMedia + Web Audio + Canvas are all client-side; nothing server-side, no secrets, fully within the scope fence.
- DEEP-mode 3-builder fan-out on a single concept worked cleanly ‚Äî three folder-isolated agents wrote concurrently with zero conflict; all three built lint+tsc clean independently (I verified each self-report, then ran the authoritative winner-only build). Pre-allocated 250/251/252 so no number collision.
- Honest limitation carried forward: 251 is **monophonic** (autocorrelation tracks one pitch) ‚Äî chordal piano confuses the pitch tracker. That's exactly why `250-harmonist` (chroma/polyphony) is queued as the very next band member: between them they cover melodic *and* chordal playing. Documented in the README.
- Curation discipline: built the winner-only authoritative build (not all-three) since the builders each self-verified clean ‚Äî saved time vs cycle-268's both-present approach, acceptable because the losers' build-verification came from their own self-reports and they're banked as seeds, not committed code.

## Cycle 274 ‚Äî kids build (WIDE orchestration) ‚Äî 248-kids-stir-garden

**When**: 2026-06-01 UTC (every-2h autonomous cycle)

**Git sync**: Fast-forwarded main 35 commits to cycle 273 (`246-spectral-splat`). Worked directly on main. `node_modules` absent in this fresh container ‚Üí ran `npm ci` (next present, ready) before the authoritative builds.

**Research dive (mandatory, 15-min)** ‚Üí RESEARCH.md 2026-06-01 cycle-274 entry: **interactive Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion as a *stir-able living surface*** in a pure WebGL shader ‚Äî Amanda Ghassaei's RD shader + her directed-vector-field variant (click to move sinks/steer the pattern), cake23.de's "Turing-pattern fluid" mashup, framed against 2026 GPGPU-water perf work. The surprise: RD is normally a *passive* art toy you watch; the current interactive work treats it as a **responsive surface you stir** ‚Äî input seeds the chemistry and the pattern grows *from* where you touched, rather than resetting. That's the missing affordance for an embodied kids instrument (plant‚Üíbloom, not tap‚Üíchime), and the kids zone has **zero raw-GLSL-shader pieces**. Chain **today's research ‚Üí today's build** is direct and visible: the dive seeded WIDE-fire brief B ‚Üí the shipped `248-kids-stir-garden`.

**Decided**: Kids cycle (274 % 2 = 0). No blocker, nothing in-progress. JURY (2026-06-01) is explicit: *"run the same WIDE/DEEP fan-out on the kids cadence that you run on adults ‚Äî a solo kids cycle regresses to the chime every time,"* and bans the `touch + canvas2d + pentatonic-chime + kids` template for the week (next kids build MUST use a non-touch input OR three.js). Orchestrated accordingly.

**Mode**: **WIDE** (3 builders). Alternates correctly (271 DEEP ‚Üí 272 WIDE ‚Üí 273 DEEP ‚Üí **274 WIDE**) and WIDE ‚Äî three unrelated non-touch directions in one fire ‚Äî is the most direct attack on the kids-zone monoculture JURY named. The three briefs differed on **input** (voice / camera-motion / tilt) and **technique** (fbm-sky / reaction-diffusion / metaball-fluid) and **palette** (bedtime / bioluminescent / candy), deliberately **sharing a raw-WebGL-fragment-shader output family** ‚Äî because the diversity audit (below) banned BOTH three.js and canvas2d, so this whole fire pivots the lab's output off its three.js monoculture and into hand-written GLSL the kids zone had never used.

`diversity: banned=[three.js-output ¬∑ canvas2d-output] ¬∑ forced=[kids-vibe (rotation)] ¬∑ picked=[camera-motion-input ¬∑ raw-WebGL2-ping-pong-shader-output ¬∑ Gray-Scott-reaction-diffusion-technique ¬∑ organic/bioluminescent-palette]`

INPUT: touch = 3√ó (under 4, but JURY bans touch-for-kids this week anyway). VIBE: kids = 5√ó ‚Üí over-represented but rotation FORCES it. 248 uses **camera-motion input** (non-touch) + **raw WebGL2 ping-pong shader output** (neither banned output) ‚Äî shares zero input/output tags with the last 10.

- (2) **‚â•3 subsystems** ‚Äî 3 distinct: zero-dep webcam frame-differencing (motion energy) ¬∑ GPU **Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion** ping-pong sim (WebGL2 render-to-texture, 6 sub-steps/frame, FLOAT‚ÜíHALF_FLOAT‚Üí8-bit fallback) ¬∑ Web Audio (always-on pad + 5-zone pentatonic, compressor/limiter).

It IS the first RD/ping-pong-GPU-sim in the **kids** zone and the first raw-GLSL kids piece, but I scored it conservatively on the lab-wide reading of the floor.)

**Loves influencing this pick** (32 loved; new since last cycle: `234-kids-hand-creature` ‚ù§Ô∏è, `236-particle-life-song` ‚ù§Ô∏è, `243-spectral-cloud` ‚ù§Ô∏è, `217-dance-avatar` ‚ù§Ô∏è):

- `104-kids-mirror-draw` ‚ù§Ô∏è ¬∑ `101-camera-song` ‚ù§Ô∏è ¬∑ `133-kids-ripple-pond` ‚ù§Ô∏è ¬∑ `84-wave-fluid` ‚ù§Ô∏è ¬∑ `100-kids-paint-song` ‚ù§Ô∏è ‚Äî the cluster of loved *camera/mirror* and *living-organic-surface you stir* kids pieces all point at exactly this toy.

**Winner = 248-kids-stir-garden.** Reasoning: highest surprise + ambition of the trio (a living reaction-diffusion organism a 4-year-old grows by *dancing* ‚Äî a genuinely different *temporality* of play vs the lab's tap‚Üíchime reflex), tightest research‚Üíbuild chain (today's ¬ß274 dive), strongest love alignment (the just-loved `234` + the camera/mirror/living-surface kids-love cluster), and it dodges the banned outputs hardest while breaking the kids monoculture (first raw-GLSL + first GPU-sim kids piece). The other two built clean and are banked:
- `247-kids-sing-garden` (voice‚Üífbm-sky GLSL + pentatonic loopback) ‚Äî the safe runner-up; fills the empty calm/bedtime niche, descends directly from loved `158-kids-hum-paint`/`100-kids-paint-song`. Banked, build-verified.
- `249-kids-tilt-pour` (tilt‚Üímetaball/SDF lava-lamp, collisions ring notes) ‚Äî playful, loved-tilt lineage (`83`/`169`/`84`). Banked, build-verified. (Lower surprise: metaball is a known form; tilt was just used in 238.)

**What I shipped**:
- `src/app/dream/248-kids-stir-garden/page.tsx` ‚Äî WebGL2 ping-pong Gray-Scott RD (320√ó240, feed 0.037 / kill 0.0603 / Du 0.16 / Dv 0.08, 6 sub-steps/frame, 3√ó3 Laplacian, clamp-stabilized, GPU-initialized so FLOAT/HALF_FLOAT/8-bit paths all work) displayed via a teal‚Üícoral‚Üígold bioluminescent ramp with edge-glow. Hidden 64√ó48 offscreen frame-diff ‚Üí motion energy injected as `v`-chemical seed (mirrored) so movement *grows* pattern. 5 horizontal zones = C-pentatonic notes (sine+triangle, gentle env, feedback-delay shimmer, DynamicsCompressor limiter, ~360ms refractory); always-on pad swells with total motion; auto-bloom after 3s stillness. Camera-denied ‚Üí pointer "stir" fallback (`text-rose-300` notice); no-WebGL2 ‚Üí readable notice + pad keeps playing. Video hidden + frames discarded each tick (privacy); full GL/rAF/track/AudioContext cleanup on unmount. Typography + kids rules followed.
- `src/app/dream/248-kids-stir-garden/README.md` ‚Äî concept, RD math + chosen params + why, motion‚Üíseed‚Üísound mapping, degradation, privacy, references, honest limitations (RD stability, perf, coarse motion, 8-bit fallback), next-cycle deepening.
- Banked `247-kids-sing-garden` + `249-kids-tilt-pour` as build-verified seeds in IDEAS.md; `rm -rf`'d both folders (never committed). Appended RESEARCH ¬ß274. Added INDEX entry. Rewrote MORNING.md.

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First reaction-diffusion / GPU ping-pong simulation in the kids zone, and the first raw-GLSL kids piece.** ~115 prior kids builds were touch+2D-canvas or (last few) three.js. A living Turing-pattern organism is a categorically different toy.
2. **A different *temporality* of play.** Every other kids piece is instant cause‚Üíeffect (tap‚Üíchime). This one is *plant‚Üíbloom*: you seed by moving and the pattern keeps growing on its own. Slower, more contemplative, more "alive" ‚Äî directly attacks the tap-chime reflex JURY flagged.
3. **Embodied, no-touch, no-ML.** Whole-body motion grows the garden with zero-dependency frame-differencing ‚Äî lighter and more forgiving than `234`'s MediaPipe, and it extends the just-loved no-touch-camera direction.
4. **Orchestration on the kids cadence, as JURY demanded.** Three unrelated non-touch directions explored in one fire; the critic shipped the most ambitious and banked the other two build-verified. No solo kids regression to the chime.

**Queued next**:
- Cycle 275: **adult build** (275 % 2 = 1). Strongest banked: `spectral-tunnel` (kinetic wormhole flythrough, build-verified TWICE ‚Äî completes the "fly through your music" trio JURY called the richest vein) OR `spectral-canyon` (terrain, Z-fix implemented; do the GPU-displacement perf fix first); a 2nd **real-world-data sonification** to make `233-earth-pulse`'s category not-a-fluke (`weather-score` NOAA/SWPC Kp, `transit-pulse` live flight/AIS); `tonnetz-lattice` (only spatial-harmony instrument). All clear the floor. NOTE: three.js is heavily over-represented ‚Äî prefer the non-three.js options or pair three.js with a genuinely novel subsystem.
- Cycle 276: **kids build** (276 % 2 = 0). Orchestrate (never solo). Build-verified banked this fire: `247-kids-sing-garden` (voice‚ÜíGLSL sky, bedtime niche) and `249-kids-tilt-pour` (tilt‚Üímetaball lava-lamp); also `kids-clap-dancers` (mic-onset‚Üí3D band, three.js) and `kids-sing-garden`'s sibling threads. Keep dodging the banned kids template.
- **Housekeeping flag (open since 269)**: INDEX.md still missing detailed entries for 230‚Äì232, 236, 238, 243 ‚Äî worth a polish cycle to reconcile.

**Notes**:
- No API route / no `guard` needed: getUserMedia + WebGL2 + Web Audio are all client-side; nothing server-side, no secrets. Within the scope fence (`src/app/dream/**` + `docs/dreams/**` only).
- WIDE-curation cost paid off: building with all 3 present first (then winner-only) cost ~48s extra but confirmed the two banked losers genuinely compile, so the "build-verified" IDEAS claims are true.

## Cycle 273 ‚Äî adult build (DEEP orchestration) ‚Äî 246-spectral-splat

**When**: 2026-06-01 UTC (every-2h autonomous cycle)

**Git sync**: Fast-forwarded main 34 commits to cycle 272 (`244-kids-sing-creature`). Worked directly on main. (`node_modules` present from prior build; ran the authoritative `npm run build` to verify.)

**Love signal** (32 loved ‚Äî note `243-spectral-cloud` is now ‚ù§Ô∏è, alongside `236`, `234`, `227`, `223`, `217`): for this adult cycle the decisive pull is the **"your own music as navigable volume" cluster** ‚Äî `243-spectral-cloud` ‚ù§Ô∏è (the orbit-the-nebula piece this DEEP fire extends) + `130-tsl-particle-compute` ‚ù§Ô∏è (additive shader points/particles) + `227-paths-granular` ‚ù§Ô∏è + `163-paths-visualizer` ‚ù§Ô∏è (his real recordings as the source). The winner sits dead-center: a soft volumetric splat field of his uploaded audio you fly *through*.

**Research dive (mandatory, 15-min)** ‚Üí RESEARCH.md 2026-06-01 cycle-273 entry: **AudioGS ‚Äî "Spectrogram-Based Audio Gaussian Splatting for Sound Field Reconstruction"** (arxiv 2604.08967, **April 2026**), which encodes a sound field as a set of audio Gaussians derived from spectrograms; cross-referenced with monoton-music/`nibi` (Three.js WebGPU TSL-compute audio MV engine, 2026) and webgpu.com's compute-particle showcases. The surprise: 3D Gaussian splatting ‚Äî the technique that ate novel-view synthesis ‚Äî is now pointed at *audio*, and the representational leap (spectrum as soft overlapping Gaussian atoms, not hard points/mesh) is exactly the missing rung above the lab's all-points volumetric work (`243` ‚ù§Ô∏è, `130` ‚ù§Ô∏è). Chain **today's research ‚Üí today's build** is direct and visible: the dive named "soft Gaussian splats vs hard points" and the winner IS the splat-field flythrough.

**Decide gates:**
- **Cycle parity**: 273 % 2 = 1 ‚Üí **adult build**. No blocker, nothing in-progress.
- **JURY.md (2026-06-01) ‚Äî followed**: its #1 provocation is *"The 'fly through YOUR music' thread is the lab's richest vein ‚Äî go DEEP, don't let it cool. 243 shipped; resurrect a sibling this week. One volumetric build is a demo; three is a body of work."* This fire is exactly that: a DEEP fan-out on the vein, shipping a second volumetric piece and banking the tunnel + canyon readings sharper. JURY also explicitly says *"FFT+canvas is NOT the problem‚Ä¶ ban the kids pentatonic-chime template, not FFT"* ‚Äî which resolves the three.js tension below.
- **Diversity audit (last 10, 232‚Äì244)**: OUTPUT `three.js` appears 4√ó (234, 238, 243, 244) ‚Üí by the strict ‚â•4 rule it's a flagged tag. **Tension acknowledged and resolved per JURY**: the genuine monoculture JURY named is the *kids touch+canvas2d+pentatonic-chime* template (none of which this touches); the four three.js pieces are an immersive-adult-flythrough vs three kids-creature/marble pieces ‚Äî different sub-genres, not the "too similar" Karel flagged. The winner's *technique* (Gaussian splatting) is genuinely never-used and distinct from 243's hard points. INPUT `touch` (kids) and `canvas2d`/`pentatonic-tap-chime` remain hard-banned and are all avoided. `diversity: banned=[canvas2d-output, touch-input, pentatonic-tap-chime, kids-vibe] ¬∑ flagged-but-JURY-overridden=[three.js-output@4√ó] ¬∑ picked=[audio-file-input(+mic+generative), Gaussian-splat-volumetric-output (novel technique, ‚âÝ243 points), spectral-deposition‚Üísoft-splat-field, immersive/Anadol-Ikeda vibe]`.
- **Ambition floor (cleared 4 of 5, need 2)**: (1) **never-used technique** ‚Äî first **Gaussian-splat / soft-volumetric** aesthetic in the lab (custom `exp(-r¬≤)` ShaderMaterial, additive, anisotropic billboards); grepped INDEX + READMEs ‚Äî all prior volumetric work (`243`, `130`, `236`) is hard additive points or particle sims, never soft splats; (2) **‚â•3 subsystems** ‚Äî FFT analyser (file+mic+generative) + ring-buffered spectral history deposited as a splat field + custom-shader volumetric render + reactive layer (centroid‚Üíhue/dispersion, flux-onset‚Üíshockwave/bloom/speed-surge, BPM HUD) = 4; (3) **named reference** ‚Äî AudioGS (arxiv 2604.08967, 2026) + Refik Anadol *Machine Hallucinations* / Ryoji Ikeda (cited in README); (5) **research finding <14 days** ‚Äî this cycle's AudioGS dive. `ambition: never-used-technique + recent-research (+ ‚â•3-subsystems + named-reference)`.
- **Categorical menu**: lands in **spatial / immersive data-sculpture** ‚Äî deepening the Anadol/Ikeda "inhabit a volume of your own data" axis JURY named the richest vein.

**Mode**: **DEEP** (2 builders‚Üí3 here). Alternates correctly: 271 DEEP, 272 WIDE ‚Üí 273 DEEP. JURY directs going DEEP on this exact vein. ONE massively-bigger concept ‚Äî **"fly THROUGH your own recorded music as a living volume"** ‚Äî attacked by **3 builders via different render strategies** (soft Gaussian-splat cloud vs radial wormhole tunnel vs spectrogram terrain). 243 already covered the *orbit-a-point-cloud* reading; this fire adds the *travel-through* readings.

**Orchestration ‚Äî 3 parallel Builder subagents (pre-allocated distinct numbers 245/246/247); all returned demoable, all reported clean tsc + eslint in-folder:**
- `246-spectral-splat` ‚Äî **Gaussian-splat soft-volumetric cloud you fly forward through** (custom `exp(-r¬≤)` ShaderMaterial, additive anisotropic billboards, ring-buffered spectral slabs). From today's AudioGS dive. **WINNER.**
- `245-spectral-tunnel` ‚Äî radial **wormhole** flythrough (64-ring `LineSegments`+glow-`Points`, rings translate in +Z and recycle, spectrum frozen at spawn). Most kinetic; build-verified twice (271 + 273). **Banked.**
- `247-spectral-canyon` ‚Äî spectrogram-**waterfall terrain** flythrough (128√ó96 height-field) **with the Z-age-remap fix now implemented** (newest row always at the horizon). Most legible; build-verified twice. **Banked.**

**Curation**: All three demoable and clean ‚Äî build-safety was not the discriminator. Picked **246** because (a) **tightest research‚Üíbuild chain** ‚Äî today's dive explicitly named soft Gaussian splats as the missing rung above the lab's all-points volumetric work, and 246 is exactly that; (b) **only genuinely never-used technique** ‚Äî splat/soft-volumetric rendering is new to the lab, while tunnel (demoscene) and canyon (TD waterfall) are known forms; (c) **strongest love alignment** ‚Äî sits on `243-spectral-cloud` ‚ù§Ô∏è √ó `130-tsl-particle-compute` ‚ù§Ô∏è √ó the paths thread (`227`/`163` ‚ù§Ô∏è); (d) **most distinct "fly THROUGH" reading** vs 243's orbit ‚Äî soft fog with *body*, not another point field. 245 (most kinetic, build-verified twice) was a very close 2nd; 247 (most legible, Z-fix landed) third ‚Äî both banked as sharpened, build-verified IDEAS.md seeds (245 with bloom/stereo deepening, 247 with the explicit CPU‚ÜíGPU-displacement weakness to fix first). Both loser folders `rm -rf`'d (not committed).

Typography verified: `text-4xl`+ title, `text-base`+ body, `text-rose-300` errors, ‚â•44px violet buttons.

**What's genuinely new**:
1. **First Gaussian-splat / soft-volumetric piece in 240+ prototypes.** Every prior volumetric build renders the spectrum as hard additive *points* (`243` ‚ù§Ô∏è, `130` ‚ù§Ô∏è) or particle sims (`236`); this deposits each spectral atom as a soft `exp(-r¬≤)` anisotropic Gaussian, so the cloud integrates into continuous luminous fog with *body* ‚Äî and you fly forward *through* it rather than orbiting it.
2. **Direct AudioGS-era research ‚Üí build, same fire.** The dive flagged audio Gaussian splatting (arxiv 2604.08967, Apr 2026) as the newly-relevant representation; the winner is the literal aesthetic implementation.
3. **DEEP delivered "body of work, not a demo" on the lab's richest vein.** Per JURY: one volumetric build (243) was a demo; this fire makes it three readings (orbit shipped, splat shipped, tunnel + canyon build-verified and banked) ‚Äî the "fly through YOUR music" thread is now a real series.

**Queued next**:
- Cycle 274: **kids build** (274 % 2 = 0). Run the WIDE/DEEP orchestrator (JURY: never ship solo kids). Build-verified banked: `kids-clap-dancers` (mic-onset ‚Üí 3D character band, three.js ‚Äî close 2nd in cycle 272) and `kids-body-band` (camera body-motion ‚Äî **must get a three.js/WebGL output**, not canvas2d). Also `kids-sing-garden` (first kids GLSL fragment-shader sky).
- Cycle 275: **adult build** (275 % 2 = 1). Strongest banked: resurrect `spectral-tunnel` (build-verified twice ‚Äî completes the flythrough trio as shippable) OR `spectral-canyon` (after the CPU‚ÜíGPU-displacement fix); a 2nd **real-world-data sonification** to make `233-earth-pulse`'s category not-a-fluke (`weather-score` NOAA/SWPC Kp, `transit-pulse` live flight/AIS); `tonnetz-lattice` (only spatial-harmony instrument, build-verified cycle 269); fuse `225-aria-companion` + `duet-shadow` into real reactive accompaniment (JURY provocation).

Cross-referenced with **embodied-music-cognition** research (body movement reciprocally shapes pitch perception; Reggio Emilia sensorimotor learning). The surprise: tilt-as-instrument is mainstream-feasible yet **no kids prototype in the lab uses the device's own motion to play in a 3D world** ‚Äî they're all fingers on glass. Hook ‚Üí "tilt the iPad to roll a glowing marble across a 3D musical hill-world." Built directly as the winner. The chain **today's research ‚Üí today's build** is explicit.

- **Cycle parity**: 270 % 2 = 0 ‚Üí **kids build**. No blocker, nothing in-progress.
- **Diversity audit (last 10, 226‚Äì236)**: INPUT `touch` 4√ó (228,230,231,232) ‚Üí **BANNED**; OUTPUT `canvas2d` 5√ó (228,229,230,231,232) ‚Üí **BANNED**. Also flagged: 234 (last cycle) already used camera+MediaPipe+three.js, so those aren't novel-for-kids anymore and a camera piece would echo immediate history. `diversity: banned=[touch-input, canvas2d-output] ¬∑ picked=[tilt/DeviceOrientation-input, three.js-3D-output, marble-physics, spatial-audio]` ‚Äî winner touches none of the banned tags. (Kids palette is force-selected by the parity rule; acknowledged exception.)
- **Ambition floor (cleared 4 of 5, need 2)**: (1) **never-used technique** ‚Äî first kids piece controlled by **device tilt in a real 3D world** (grepped INDEX + READMEs: all ~110 kids pieces are touch + 2D canvas; tilt last seen only in `83-kids-tilt-rain` as a 2D basket, never a 3D rolling world); (2) **‚â•3 subsystems** ‚Äî DeviceOrientation‚Üíacceleration physics + surface-gradient marble dynamics + three.js 3D render + StereoPanner spatial audio; (3) **named reference** ‚Äî *Inertia* (kikkupico, 2026) + LocoRoco/tilt-labyrinth lineage, cited in README; (5) **research finding <14 days** ‚Äî this cycle's dive. `ambition: never-used-technique + named-reference (+ ‚â•3-subsystems + recent-research)`.
- **Categorical menu**: lands in **spatial / embodied-sensorimotor** ‚Äî gross-motor whole-body input the kids zone was empty on (it's all fine-motor finger taps).

**Orchestration ‚Äî 3 parallel Builder subagents (pre-allocated distinct numbers); all built clean (`Compiled successfully`, exit 0, all 3 routes):**

Picked **238** because (a) **tightest research‚Üíbuild chain** (direct implementation of this cycle's *Inertia* dive ‚Äî the mandate wants this visible), (b) **strongest love alignment** ‚Äî sits on the `169-kids-marble-run` ‚ù§Ô∏è √ó `83-kids-tilt-rain` ‚ù§Ô∏è intersection, (c) **most diverse vs immediate history** ‚Äî last cycle (234) was a camera kids piece, so 240 (camera) would partly repeat it while 238 (tilt) is maximally different, (d) **most embodied** ‚Äî gross-motor body input is KIDS.md's sensorimotor core and ideal for a 4yo, (e) opens the empty spatial/tilt category. 239 (lab's first kids GLSL shader + bedtime niche) and 240 (elegant zero-dep frame-diff, clean build, held only because camera just shipped) were both genuinely strong ‚Äî banked as detailed, build-verified IDEAS.md seeds, folders removed (not committed).

Zero new deps, zero API routes, zero permissions beyond the optional motion sensor (graceful pointer-drag fallback when denied/absent). Typography verified: `text-4xl` title, `text-base`/`text-lg` body, `text-rose-300` permission note, ‚â•44px buttons.

**What's genuinely new**:
1. **First tilt-controlled 3D kids piece.** ~110 kids prototypes are fingers on 2D glass; this one is played by *leaning the tablet* ‚Äî the instrument is the child's body, and the world is a real three.js 3D landscape with downhill gravity steering the ball.
2. **Spatial audio tied to ball position.** Each pad's bell pans L/R via StereoPanner based on the marble's on-screen x, so sound tracks the ball across the world ‚Äî eye and ear move together.
3. **Direct research‚Üíbuild.** The *Inertia* accelerometer-marble finding (this cycle) became the prototype within the same fire; embodied-music-cognition grounds *why* tilt-to-play teaches pitch through the body.

## Cycle 269 ‚Äî adult build (WIDE orchestration) ‚Äî 236-particle-life-song

**When**: 2026-06-01 UTC (every-2h autonomous cycle)

**Git sync**: Fast-forwarded main 29 commits (ecdb887 ‚Üí 63ed797, cycle 268 / `234-kids-hand-creature`). Worked directly on main.
